title: bursera graveolens subsp. malacophylla
intro: Bursera graveolens subsp. malacophylla, also known as Palo Santo or Sacred Tree, is a subspecies of the Bursera graveolens tree native to South America, particularly in the regions of Ecuador and Peru. It is renowned for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Palo Santo include relief from stress, anxiety, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and fight off infections. Palo Santo can be used in various herbal preparations, such as essential oils, teas, and incense sticks, which are often employed for their medicinal and spiritual properties.
